element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ground-based item throwing mechanic alongside melee
Character-specific projectile or energy beam attacks; Captain America uses shield throws, Hawkeye uses arrows, Iron Man and Vision use energy beams
Captain America and Hawkeye perform flying kicks during jumps
Iron Man and Vision fire energy beams at 45-degree angles during jumps
Side-scrolling flying and shooting; Iron Man and Vision fly independently, Captain America and Hawkeye use flying machines
Sequential encounters with Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper, Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, Controller, Crossbones, culminating in Red Skull
Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, and Namor the Sub-Mariner appear to aid player characters

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching safe zones between boss encounters
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade-style intensity with escalating villain difficulty; side-scrolling combat sections punctuated by flying sequences create rhythm variation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic melee combat against generic henchmen; introduction to hand-to-hand and throwable items
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive introduction of special attacks, flying sequences, and mid-tier supervillains (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper)
difficulty_curve.late_game: High-difficulty supervillain encounters (Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, Controller, Crossbones) leading to final Red Skull confrontation
notable_spikes: First named villain (Klaw) - escalation from generic enemies
Introduction of flying sequences - new mechanical mastery required
Ultron encounter - significant power spike in mid-late progression
Red Skull final boss - ultimate difficulty peak

notable_level_patterns.description: Typical structure involves fighting multiple generic enemy waves before facing a named supervillain; this pattern repeats throughout the game with escalating difficulty
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Progression through Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind encounters

notable_level_patterns.description: Occasional departure from side-scrolling ground combat into flying and shooting sequences; provides mechanical variety and pacing relief
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Iron Man and Vision's inherent flying, Captain America and Hawkeye's flying machine sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Each playable character (Captain America, Iron Man, Hawkeye, Vision) has distinct combat approaches: different projectile types (shield vs arrows vs energy beams) and different aerial attack patterns (flying kick vs angled beam)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Available throughout game; impacts level strategy based on character selection

sources_used: wikipedia: Captain America and The Avengers
